channel.title: GrantOtter — Federal Funding Updates
channel.description: Aggregated weekly feed of grant opportunities and agency news from NIH, NSF, and DARPA.

item.title: Early Career Faculty (ECF) 2025 Awards
item.description: Back to ECF Home Advanced Diagnostics for High-Enthalpy Test Facilities Simulating Spacecraft Atmospheric Entry Planning for Autonomous Spacecraft Using Machine Learning Methods to Enable Onboard Guidance, Navigation, and Control

item.title: NASA Volunteers Help Zooniverse Reach 1 Billion Classifications
item.description: The Zooniverse, a NASA grantee that runs the world’s largest platform for online people-powered research, has reached an extraordinary milestone: 1 billion classifications contributed by volunteers around the world. This milestone is a celebration of everyone who has marked a dip in a light curve, confirmed the presence of a moving object in a short […]

item.title: You’re Invited: A Conversation on NIH’s Unified Funding Strategy
item.description: We invite you to join a conversation between NIH leadership on NIH’s Unified Funding Strategy. Our Unified Funding Strategy is designed to support NIH Institutes and Centers to make informed, transparent, and flexible funding decisions. During their conversation, NIH leadership will clarify the approach’s rationale, goals, and implementation across NIH; address misconceptions; and help foster communication between NIH staff and research community.

item.title: NASA Photographer Captures Images from F-18 Over Washington
item.description: NASA flight photographers capture history from a perspective few ever experience, getting a rare bird’s-eye view of the agency’s missions in action. Their photos document key NASA research and give the public a front-row seat to the work happening behind the scenes. Jim Ross, a photographer at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, […]

item.title: Waxing Gibbous Moon
item.description: The waxing gibbous moon is nestled in the darkness of space in this June 26, 2026, image from the International Space Station. The space station was 264 miles above the Indian Ocean southeast of Madagascar at the time. The waxing gibbous phase comes before the full moon phase. During this time, the Moon appears brighter […]

item.title: Notice of Information: Implementation of HHS Annual SBIR and STTR Application/Proposal Limit
item.description: Notice NOT-OD-26-090 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Where Venezuela’s Earthquakes Shifted the Ground
item.description: Radar data from the NISAR satellite show that La Guaira and nearby areas experienced significant ground displacement from the June 2026 temblors.

item.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Anil Menon Launch to Space Station
item.description: NASA astronaut Anil Menon will launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on Tuesday, July 14, accompanied by cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina, where they will join the Expedition 74 crew advancing scientific research. Menon, Dubrov, and Kikina will lift off at 10:47 a.m. EDT (7:47 p.m. Baikonur time) […]

item.title: NASA Space Telescope Maps Magnetic Fields of ‘Lighthouse’ Pulsar
item.description: For the first time, scientists have used NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) to directly measure the magnetic fields of PSR J1101−6101, a pulsar located within what is often referred to as the Lighthouse Nebula. The results provide new insight into the structure of some of the most extreme objects in the cosmos, as NASA […]

item.title: Principal Investigator and Quality Assessment Reports Evaluate Umbra Synthetic Aperture Radar Data
item.description: The reports add to the growing documentation on commercial data’s contributions to Earth science research and applications.

item.title: September 7 (Monday), 2026: NIH Closed for the Federal Holiday
item.description: NIH (including help desks) will be closed on Monday, September 7, 2026 for the federal holiday (Labor Day).

item.title: Curiosity Sees Martian Sulfur Up Close
item.description: This close-up view shows fragments of sulfur crystals — the first ever seen on the Red Planet. The crystals were found after NASA’s Curiosity Mars rover happened to drive over a rock and crush it on May 30, 2024. Several days later, Curiosity used a camera on the end of its robotic arm to take […]

item.title: NASA Scientists Take to Air and Space to Study Arctic Sea Ice
item.description: This month, engineers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California are testing a spacecraft sensor that will help measure how quickly Arctic sea ice is disappearing. And while that instrument won’t launch for another year, scientists started preparing for its use during a recent field campaign in the Canadian wilderness. Researchers spent two weeks […]
item.category: ICESat-2 (Ice, Cloud and land Elevation Satellite-2)

item.title: Declare Your Path to Research Independence Through the NIH F99/K00 Funding Opportunity: Perspectives from NIH Institutes and Centers
item.description: As we celebrate our nation’s birthday and in the spirit of charting new paths, NIH is pleased to announce a new parent funding opportunity designed to help promising Ph.D. students take an important step toward research independence.

item.title: Inviting Comments and Suggestions on Topic Areas for the NIDCR Strategic Plan for Fiscal Years 2027-2031
item.description: Notice NOT-DE-26-015 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: RAPIID - TA3
item.description: The Resuscitation and Prevention of Ischemia-Induced Dysfunction (RAPIID) program - TA3R - will focus on pushing high technology readiness level (TRL)/medical readiness level (MRL) capabilities through the regulatory process. | See Program

item.title: Meet the DARPA Lift Challenge teams
item.description: Over 120 teams to compete for $6.5 million in prizes and test novel, heavy-lift drone designs.

item.title: Notice of Change: Early Expiration of Summer Research Education Experience Program (R25 Clinical Trial Not Allowed)
item.description: Notice NOT-NS-26-040 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: NSF launches Project Triad to advance quantum technology for real-world applications
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation today announced Project Triad, a first-of-its-kind initiative to integrate quantum sensing, quantum networking and quantum computing into a single operational...

item.title: Podcast: The fight against high blood pressure [hypertension]
item.description: U.S. National Science Foundation-supported professor Tao Zhou discusses the development of a new 3D-printed hydrogel-based bioelectronic device for hypertension treatment.

item.title: Notice of Early Expiration of PAR-24-252 "Innovative Programs to Enhance Research Training (IPERT) (R25 Independent Clinical Trial Not Allowed)"
item.description: Notice NOT-GM-26-010 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: How Do I Find Contract Solicitations?
item.description: The primary method to find contract solicitations is the federal-wide System for Award Management website (SAM.gov), which lists federal contracting opportunities higher than $20,000.
